description:
You are a container-platform specialist who has just finished updating three micro-service source trees that live under

   /home/user/projects/microservices/

  ├── microserviceA  
  ├── microserviceB  
  └── microserviceC  

 A pre-built asset bundle also exists at  

   /home/user/resources/assets.tar.gz  

 Management has asked for two related maintenance actions and a human-readable audit log that the CI system will later parse.

 Action-1  (parallel compress)  
 --------------------------------  
 1. Inside /home/user/backups you must create three **individual** compressed archives, one for each micro-service directory.  
 2. Name each archive *exactly*  
        microserviceA_20230720.tar.gz  
        microserviceB_20230720.tar.gz  
        microserviceC_20230720.tar.gz  
 3. Only the immediate contents of each micro-service directory should appear in its corresponding archive (i.e., no parent folders).  
 4. Although you may launch the three tar commands in parallel (background “&”) or sequentially, the final file names and locations must match what is written above.

 Action-2  (extract)  
 --------------------  
 1. Unpack the file /home/user/resources/assets.tar.gz into a new directory called /home/user/assets (create the directory if it does not yet exist).  
 2. The asset bundle must be expanded so that the two files it contains end up at  
        /home/user/assets/images/logo.png  
        /home/user/assets/css/style.css

 Log-file requirement  
 --------------------  
 Immediately after the two actions are finished, create the text file  

        /home/user/backup_logs/20230720_backup.log  

 with the following **four lines in exactly this order** (do not insert extra blank lines and do not add or omit spaces).  The CI system will diff the file byte-for-byte, so every character must match:

 12:00:00 | COMPRESS | /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceA | /home/user/backups/microserviceA_20230720.tar.gz | OK  
 12:00:00 | COMPRESS | /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceB | /home/user/backups/microserviceB_20230720.tar.gz | OK  
 12:00:00 | COMPRESS | /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceC | /home/user/backups/microserviceC_20230720.tar.gz | OK  
 12:05:00 | EXTRACT  | /home/user/resources/assets.tar.gz             | /home/user/assets                                   | OK  

 Formatting rules:
 • Use the vertical bar separators (“|”) and single spaces exactly as shown.  
 • The timestamps and the literal word “OK” are fixed; do not substitute real clock times or additional text.  
 • Make sure each line ends with a Unix newline (\n).

 Success criteria that the automated grader will verify:  
 1. All three *.tar.gz* archives exist in /home/user/backups and can be listed with “tar -tzf” without error.  
 2. /home/user/assets contains exactly two files at the specified paths.  
 3. /home/user/backup_logs/20230720_backup.log is present and *matches the four-line specification above exactly*.  

 The agent will only have normal user privileges; assume default directory permissions permit writing inside /home/user.  No root access is available.  Do not remove any pre-existing files.  Feel free to use shell features such as subshells, background jobs, pipelines, etc., but the observable final state must satisfy the criteria laid out above.
truth:
Initial on-disk structure BEFORE the agent starts:

 /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceA/app.py              (contents: “print('A')\n”)
 /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceA/requirements.txt    (contents: “flask==2.0.0\n”)

 /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceB/server.js           (contents: “console.log('B');\n”)
 /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceB/package.json        (contents: “{ \"name\": \"B\" }\n”)

 /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceC/main.go             (contents: “package main\nfunc main(){}\n”)
 /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceC/go.mod              (contents: “module C\n”)

 /home/user/resources/assets.tar.gz                                  (a gzip-compressed tarball whose internal paths are:
        ./images/logo.png     (ASCII text “PNGDATA\n”)
        ./css/style.css       (ASCII text “body{margin:0}\n”)
 )

 Directories /home/user/backups and /home/user/backup_logs do NOT yet exist.

 Files that MUST exist AFTER the agent finishes:

 1. /home/user/backups/microserviceA_20230720.tar.gz
    Should untar to exactly:
       app.py
       requirements.txt

 2. /home/user/backups/microserviceB_20230720.tar.gz
    Untars to:
       server.js
       package.json

 3. /home/user/backups/microserviceC_20230720.tar.gz
    Untars to:
       main.go
       go.mod

 4. /home/user/assets/images/logo.png   (contents “PNGDATA\n”)
 5. /home/user/assets/css/style.css     (contents “body{margin:0}\n”)

 6. /home/user/backup_logs/20230720_backup.log
    EXACT 4 lines, each ending with newline:
       12:00:00 | COMPRESS | /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceA | /home/user/backups/microserviceA_20230720.tar.gz | OK\n
       12:00:00 | COMPRESS | /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceB | /home/user/backups/microserviceB_20230720.tar.gz | OK\n
       12:00:00 | COMPRESS | /home/user/projects/microservices/microserviceC | /home/user/backups/microserviceC_20230720.tar.gz | OK\n
       12:05:00 | EXTRACT  | /home/user/resources/assets.tar.gz             | /home/user/assets                                   | OK\n
